title: What this project is
description: Start with the README for the research question — do safety changes in 4-bit quantized models reflect true alignment shifts or capability degradation? — and CLAUDE.md for how the repo governs itself (PROJECT_LOG as source of truth, claim-lock discipline).

title: The experiment, declared as config
description: Every run starts from YAML validated by a Pydantic schema: five model pairs sharing a model_id (baseline vs 4-bit), three benchmarks, greedy decoding, seed 42. tc1_512.yaml is the PRIMARY study config (D41).

title: Loading models — where quantization happens
description: HFModelLoader resolves dtype/device and applies BitsAndBytes NF4 (or INT8) on the fly at load; a guard hard-fails if quantization silently didn't engage. TextGenerator then does deterministic chat-templated batch generation.

title: The three benchmark lenses
description: All plugins implement one ABC: HarmBench measures harmful compliance (ASR), XSTest measures over-refusal on benign prompts, MMLU anchors capability. Each scores its own responses.

title: Running: single run to full matrix
description: run_quant_benchmark orchestrates one model-benchmark run (seed, load, generate, score, write raw.jsonl + summary.json); run_quant_matrix loads each model once for all its benchmarks and releases GPU memory between models.

title: Judge scoring — the primary ASR
description: The official HarmBench 13B classifier is the primary HarmBench scorer (D16): it re-scores raw outputs into redacted judge sidecars (IDs + booleans only), never touching immutable raw artifacts. Agreement stats quantify how much the regex proxy over-counts.

title: From numbers to claims
description: Pairwise deltas get interpretation labels (alignment_degradation vs capability_collapse_masquerading_as_safety, etc.); multiple_comparisons applies McNemar + BH-FDR across all contrasts; the genlen study showed the 128-token headline was a truncation artefact — at 512 no significant ASR increase survives anywhere.

title: Running on the TC1 cluster
description: Jobs are generated as per-model sbatch scripts (offline env, bootstrap block preserved), submitted under strict QoS limits; prefetch_tc1 caches models/datasets on the head node so compute nodes run fully offline.

title: Exploring results — the dashboard
description: The Streamlit app presents judge-primary results (rebuilt from multiple_comparisons.json, NOT the v2-proxy interpretations), lets you add a model pair via a validated form, and launches runs; data.py is the tested, Streamlit-free data layer.

title: The deliverables — report & thesis, claim-locked
description: The docx report/thesis are generated from JS builders (never hand-edited); verify_report_claims machine-checks ~55 numbers and citations against primary artifacts on every pytest run, and number_bible_512 prints the single source for every headline value.

title: The agent harness — the repo that maintains itself
description: A policy file drives machine checks: immutable-artifact manifests, CLAUDE/AGENTS sync, PROJECT_LOG discipline, stale-claim and redaction scans, report freshness. agent_check is the gate; renderers produce handoff/dashboard/checklist docs.

title: How it's all verified
description: The test suite proves each layer: end-to-end pipeline smoke, delta/interpretation math, judge redaction and idempotency, INT8 scoping guards, dashboard data invariants, and harness self-evaluation on fixture repos.
